     The Numbers Business Report

Saturday Night (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $9,511,315Details
International Box Office $466,589
Worldwide Box Office $9,977,904
Further financial details...

Synopsis

At 11:30pm on October 11th, 1975, a ferocious troupe of young comedians and writers changed television forever. Directed by Jason Reitman and written by Gil Kenan & Reitman, Saturday Night is the true story of what happened behind the scenes in the 90 minutes leading up to the first broadcast of Saturday Night Live. Full of humor, chaos, and the magic of a revolution that almost wasn’t, we count down the minutes in real time to the famous words: “Live from New York, it’s Saturday night!”

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$270,487 (2.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.80 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:95.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:5 opening theaters/2,336 max. theaters, 2.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $10,057,941

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2024 (Exclusive) by Sony Pictures
October 4th, 2024 (Limited) by Sony Pictures
October 11th, 2024 (Expands Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: October 21st, 2024 (Wide) (Italy)
October 31st, 2024 (Wide) (Australia)
November 7th, 2024 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 31st, 2025 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
March 6th, 2025 (Wide), released as Saturday Night: La Noche Que Cambió La Comedia (Mexico)
Video Release: November 12th, 2024 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, sexual references, some drug use and brief graphic nudity
Running Time: 109 minutes
Filming location (city or town): New York, NY
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Georgia, United States, New York, United States
Geographical setting: New York, New York City

Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

At 11:30pm on October 11th, 1975, a ferocious troupe of young comedians and writers changed television forever. Directed by Jason Reitman and written by Gil Kenan & Reitman, Saturday Night is the true story of what happened behind the scenes in the 90 minutes leading up to the first broadcast of Saturday Night Live. Full of humor, chaos, and the magic of a revolution that almost wasn’t, we count down the minutes in real time to the famous words: “Live from New York, it’s Saturday night!”
